description: The Olympic swimming competition venue, a unique membrane structure building, now serves as the Ice Cube Ice Sports Center.
One of China's oldest surviving imperial gardens, with the White Pagoda as its iconic structure.
A commercial district with over 500 years of history, preserving the traditional hutong style of old Beijing and its commercial culture.
The street where China's ancient highest educational institution was located, featuring well-preserved traditional architecture and a rich cultural atmosphere.
The Monument to the People's Heroes standing at the center of Tiananmen Square, a sacred memorial structure commemorating revolutionary martyrs.
A famous hutong commercial street in Beijing, featuring unique shops and local delicacies, offering an experience of traditional courtyard culture.
The ruins of a Qing Dynasty imperial garden, a historical witness to the 'Garden of Gardens,' and a patriotic education base.
The main stadium of the 2008 Olympics, a masterpiece of modern architectural art and a symbol of the Olympic spirit.
A distinctive artsy hutong in Beijing, gathering creative shops and cafes, offering an experience of old Beijing hutong culture.
One of the earliest zoos opened in China, renowned for its giant pandas and other rare animals.
A famous venue for crosstalk performances in Beijing, housed in a traditional theater building, offering an authentic crosstalk cultural experience.
A cultural and leisure street in Beijing, featuring creative shops, cafes, and specialty restaurants.
The largest mausoleum in the Ming Dynasty imperial tomb complex, the tomb of Emperor Yongle (Zhu Di).
A comprehensive exhibition of Beijing's history and culture, a modern museum with a rich collection of cultural relics.
An internationally renowned contemporary art district, a creative cultural park transformed from former industrial factories.
A historic and cultural district in Beijing, blending hutong scenery with lakeside views, with enchanting night scenes.
The main venue area of the 2008 Olympics, home to iconic structures such as the Bird's Nest and the Water Cube.
A model of ancient Chinese imperial gardens, a UNESCO World Heritage Site centered around Kunming Lake and Longevity Hill.
